---
title: "Plotly Python reference — 3D surface"
description: "Static-interactive 3D surface plot using plotly.graph_objects."
---

Reference implementation of a 3D surface plot using Plotly Python. Quarto's Jupyter engine executes the cell at build time and embeds an interactive (rotation, zoom, hover) HTML snippet into the rendered page.

```{python}
#| label: fig-cobb-douglas-surface
#| fig-cap: "Cobb-Douglas utility surface, u(x, y) = x^α · y^(1−α)."
#| fig-cap-location: margin

import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go

x = np.linspace(0.1, 10, 60)
y = np.linspace(0.1, 10, 60)
X, Y = np.meshgrid(x, y)
alpha = 0.4
U = X ** alpha * Y ** (1 - alpha)

fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Surface(x=X, y=Y, z=U, colorscale="Viridis")])
fig.update_layout(
    title="u(x, y) = x^0.4 · y^0.6",
    scene=dict(xaxis_title="x", yaxis_title="y", zaxis_title="u"),
    width=600,
    height=450,
    margin=dict(l=0, r=0, b=0, t=40),
)
fig.show()
```

For Week 1's Cobb-Douglas surface project, the recommended pattern is: this 3D surface plot via Plotly Python, plus a 2D contour with draggable parameters via JSXGraph (see `../jsxgraph/`). Each tool does what it does best.
